title: Brandy Scrap Wood Storage Bed with Drawers (Queen)
summary: A queen platform storage bed built as three drawer benches — six drawers total — clad in scrap or pallet wood. A big, materials-heavy build.
description:
A queen storage bed that's really three cabinets bolted into a platform: one bench at the foot and two along the sides, each carrying drawers, for six drawers of under-bed storage in total. The show surfaces are clad in reclaimed pallet or scrap wood, which is where the rustic look comes from and how the original kept the material cost down on the visible parts.

The single most important rule is up front: rip all your plywood strips to exactly 15-3/4" wide, every one identical. The whole bed's squareness depends on it — benches built from strips that vary by even 1/16" won't line up when you tie them together. Consider building the drawer boxes from plywood ripped to 7-3/4" rather than pine 1x8s; it's cheaper, squarer, and more stable, though 1x10 boxes make it easier to nail scrap faces onto.

Build each bench as its own unit — panels to top, tie the bottoms with 1x4s (pocket holes to the outside for strength), then a separate face frame attached to the front. Clad the exposed end panels in your scrap wood. Then tie the three benches together into the bed footprint, run slats across supports tied into the backs, and consult your mattress for slat spacing. Drawers go in set back 3/4" for the faces; a spacer block resting under each slide keeps all six drawers at a consistent height. Attach the faces last, with the drawer sitting upright so the slide play settles where it will in use.

tools.note: Every plywood strip must be ripped to exactly 15-3/4" — this is non-negotiable for square.
Cladding the scrap-wood siding and drawer faces.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4", 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-3/4"-wide strips — all rips exactly the same width.

materials.name: 1x8, 8 ft
materials.note: Drawer boxes — plywood ripped to 7-3/4" is a cheaper, squarer alternative.

materials.name: Pallet or scrap wood for siding and drawer faces
materials.note: Reclaimed; drawer faces can also be 1x12s.

cutList.note: Cut to fit opening and slides.
Any scrap wood or plywood; space per your mattress.

steps.title: Build the end bench box
steps.body: Building the foot (end) bench first: attach the side panels and middle panel to the top plywood piece, making sure the center panel is exactly centered — use the 1x4 face-frame pieces from the next step as spacers to locate it. All plywood must be your uniform 15-3/4" strips or the box won't be square.

steps.title: Tie in the bench bottom
steps.body: Tie the bottom of the bench together with 1x4s. Keep the pocket holes on the outside — easier to drive and stronger.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame separately, then attach the completed frame to the bench. The insides sit flush on the sides, the top flush to the top, and the bottom 1x4s align with the tie-ins.

steps.title: Clad the end panel
steps.body: Cover the exposed end panel of the bench in scrap or pallet boards — any wood works here. Nail and glue it on. Repeat the whole bench sequence for the two side benches.
materials: Pallet or scrap wood for siding and drawer faces

steps.title: Tie the three benches together
steps.body: Arrange the end bench and two side benches into the bed footprint and tie them together with 1x4s or 1x3s (scrap plywood strips work too — these just keep the benches aligned). It's easiest to do this with the benches positioned as they'll sit.

steps.title: Add the slat supports and slats
steps.body: The slats rest on supports tied into the backs of the side benches. Run the slats across and space them per your mattress manufacturer's recommendation — random scrap of consistent thickness is fine for the slats themselves.

steps.title: Build the six drawers
steps.body: Build the six drawer boxes. This is where most of the time and money goes. Pocket-hole the boxes together and attach the plywood bottoms. Building boxes from 1x10 (or plywood ripped to width) gives a face that's easy to nail scrap onto, at a little added cost.

steps.title: Install drawers and attach faces
steps.body: Install each drawer inset 3/4" from the face frame (or flush with the front edge of the plywood) on its 16" slides. A spacer block cut once and rested under each slide keeps all six drawers at the same height. Finally, attach the drawer faces with the drawer sitting upright so the slide play settles where it will in use, aiming for an even gap all around. Sand and finish.
materials: Pallet or scrap wood for siding and drawer faces

images.alt: Queen platform storage bed with six drawers clad in reclaimed scrap wood
